title: Listen and Choose a Response — Daily Situations

questions.stem: Could you help me carry these books to the library?

options.A: Sure, I'm heading that way anyway.
options.C: I read that book last semester.

questions.explanation: The speaker is requesting help, so a willing agreement is the most natural response.

questions.stem: I was really hoping to get into that photography workshop, but it's full.

options.A: You should take a photography class.
options.B: That's too bad. Maybe next semester.
options.C: The workshop is in the art building.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing disappointment, so a sympathetic response is most appropriate.

questions.stem: I can give you a ride to the airport if you need one.

options.A: My flight was delayed by two hours.
options.B: Are you sure you don't mind? That would be great.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering help, so a gracious acceptance is the expected response.

questions.stem: What do you think about the new cafeteria menu?

options.B: It's much better than last year's options.
options.C: The cafeteria is on the first floor.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for an opinion, so sharing a personal assessment is most appropriate.

questions.stem: I don't understand the homework assignment. Are we supposed to write a summary or an analysis?

options.A: A summary just restates the main points.
options.B: Professor Lee said it should be an analysis with your own interpretation.
options.C: The assignment is due next Friday.

questions.explanation: The speaker is confused about instructions, so clarifying the requirement is most helpful.

audioSegments.text: Could you help me carry these books to the library?
I was really hoping to get into that photography workshop, but it's full.
I can give you a ride to the airport if you need one.
What do you think about the new cafeteria menu?
I don't understand the homework assignment. Are we supposed to write a summary or an analysis?
